verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (Hsp70-1) is canonically a cytosolic/nuclear stress-inducible chaperone with no TM domain, GPI anchor, or signal peptide. However, there is extensive documented surface display on tumor cells ('membrane Hsp70') — a tumor-specific ecto-pool recognized by clinical agents (e.g., cmHsp70.1 antibody, TKD-peptide-based NK therapies, PET tracers). Surface Hsp70 is also exposed during stress, apoptosis/ICD, and on exosomes fusing with the PM. This qualifies as cell-state-induced surface display (oncogenic transformation + stress/ICD), not baseline surface residency.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Mechanism of outer-leaflet anchoring (lipid raft / Gb3 / PS binding) is debated but doesn't change the verdict.
